utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi dennis how are you today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what what brings you into the office i do n't believe i have ever seen you before here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the i'm training for a triathlon and the back of my heel hurts typically after a workout or when i first get up in the morning it will really hurt in the back of my heel all the way up into my calf

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now tell me a little bit about this triathlon training what do you are you are you running far are you tell me kind of how you work out for that today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i will run about ten klometers

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: in the morning at lunchtime i try to get on the bike for about forty five minutes to an hour

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then in the evening i go to the aquatic center and i jump in the pool and swim for about another thirty minutes to an hour

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow that's that's a that's an aggressive campaign to get there so really hope you get that triathlon in so you're how many days a week does this occur that you're training

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i do that five days a week on the weekends i kinda alternate between you know a long run and a long bike do n't really do a long swim but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i just swimming on those other five days and i take one day off

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay on your running days or when you do the running tell me a little bit about your stretching routine do you are you aggressive stretchers you do not stretch at all i know people that do both

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i do n't i do n't stretch at all i do n't have time for that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and i'm assuming that with the bicycle either you do n't really work the lower legs to get them stretched out you just jump on and go correct

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah you know i you know when i first start off i go slow until i'm warmed up and then i

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay tell me a little bit about the pain is you said it it's after the activity but it you when you first wake up in the morning it it's it's there also

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah when i first get out of bed it's just it just feels really tight and almost like it's tearing and i after i been walking around for about ten or fifteen minutes it eases up a little bit but if if i ever sit down for any extended period of time and then try to get back up it's really sore again

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: or

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no go ahead go ahead

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: or after a lot of activity it'll get sore too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay i'm sure this is impacting that ability to to really focus on your training uh as you're going with that with that type of a pain so lem me go ahead and let's let's take a look at your your and your lower extremities here and yeah okay so roll up your pants yeah okay good now does your left leg hurt at all do you get that severe pain or is it mainly on the right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no it's mainly just on the right

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay so i'm gon na just hold your leg here and i want you to take your your foot and ankle and i want you to go ahead and just pull your your toes up towards you does that create some pain when you do that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah a little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: a little bit okay now i'm just gon na hold your foot up and i want you to push against me and does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that hurts a little bit too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay okay roll over here on my my stretcher for me and i want you to dangle your your feet over the edge and what i wan na do is i just wan na i'm gon na squeeze your left calf first and when i do that does that hurt on your left side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm gon na squeeze on the right side anything there

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay well that's good your your thompson test is negative that's a really good finding and i just wan na continue pressing here on your right from your heel when i squeeze your heel does that cause significant pain at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay and if i come up a little bit further as i i run up that achilles tendon does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's real tender

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay okay great not great so yeah go ahead and sit up for me so dennis my diagnosis is achilles tendinitis and it's really from overuse when we see this frequently when somebody starts to you know get in and train aggressively for an event i'm unfortunately i'm gon na have to ask you to stop training for a a week or two i know that's concerning but i really want this to heal before we move into that next phase i did n't ask you about medication but i'm assuming whatever you were doing was n't working significantly so what i'd like you to do is take some ibuprofen or advil that's the same medication and i want you to take three tablets every six hours and that's really an anti-inflammatory see if we can eliminate some of that tendinitis type pain that you're getting that inflammation around that achilles tendon i'm gon na also order a couple physical therapy visits and the reason for that is i'd like you to get some strength and stretching understanding i think it's important for you especially here at the beginning is to get some of that real stretching knowledge in and learn how to stretch those muscles before you start these activities and they are gon na help strengthen your your lower extremities also and i want you to come back to me within you know a week to ten days following your first couple physical therapy appointments so i can monitor how you're doing i see no reason for an x-ray at this point but if this continues we're gon na you know if you're still having pain ten days in significant pain without change modification of your activity then we may have to look at more aggressive treatment plans how does that sound for you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay hey did you happen to catch that pittsburgh pang one hockey game last night

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i i was watching the track meet

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: my gosh the the pang ones are on fire you know they beat up on new york seven to two i mean the hockey's just an amazing sport i i just do you guy do you do a lot of hockey down there in town here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: used to when i lived in detroit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: oh

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hockey town but not so much anymore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no yeah detroit is a hockey town yeah the red wings okay well i'm gon na go ahead and i'll send my nurse in and go ahead and get you discharged and like i said i'd like to see you back here in seven to ten days after that first physical therapy appointment i've got a great phenomenal office staff you just call in once that gets scheduled they will get you in to that next available appointment so take care and i will talk to you later

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thanks

detail_list.summary: Dennis Martin is a 46-year-old male presenting for evaluation of right heel pain.
Right heel pain
The patient reports right heel pain starting 10 days ago during triathlon training, radiating to the calf and aggravated by activity and morning movement.
The doctor asks about the patient's triathlon training routine.
The patient runs 10 kilometers in the morning.
The patient bikes for 45 to 60 minutes at lunchtime.
The patient swims for 30 to 60 minutes in the evening.
The patient trains 5 days per week and alternates long runs and bike rides on weekends.
The patient takes one day off from exercise per week.
The doctor asks about the patient's stretching routine.
The patient does not stretch at all.
The doctor asks if the patient has any left leg pain.
The patient denies left leg pain.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the right foot and ankle, noting pain with resisted extension.
The doctor notes pain with resisted flexion during the physical exam.
The doctor performs a Thompson test on the left side.
The doctor performs a Thompson test on the right side.
The doctor notes the Thompson test is negative bilaterally and performs a calcaneal squeeze test.
The doctor notes severe tenderness to the Achilles tendon upon palpation.
The doctor assesses the patient with Achilles tendinitis due to overuse.
The doctor instructs the patient to stop training for 1 to 2 weeks.
The doctor prescribes ibuprofen 3 tablets every 6 hours.
The doctor orders physical therapy for strengthening and stretching.
The doctor schedules a follow-up visit in 7 to 10 days.
The doctor states an x-ray is not indicated at this time but may be considered if pain persists.
